problem.title: Contains Duplicate III
problem.description: Given an integer array nums and two integers indexDiff and valueDiff, return true if there are two distinct indices i and j such that abs(i - j) <= indexDiff and abs(nums[i] - nums[j]) <= valueDiff.
assumptions: Both index and value conditions must be satisfied simultaneously.

example.explanation: nums[0]=1 and nums[3]=1: |0-3|=3<=3 and |1-1|=0<=0. Use a TreeSet as a sorted sliding window: for each element check if any value in [num-t, num+t] exists in the set.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – TreeSet Sliding Window
solutions.description: Maintain a sorted TreeSet of the last indexDiff elements. For each new element x, query the set for any value in [x - valueDiff, x + valueDiff] using floor/ceiling operations. If found, return true. Then add x to the set and evict the element that has moved out of the index window. TreeSet gives O(log k) lookup per element.
